label: Quantum Physics
desc: The behaviour of matter and energy at the smallest scales — where reality gets strange, probabilistic, and deeply interconnected.

label: Neuroscience
desc: The science of the brain and nervous system — mapping the biological substrate of thought, feeling, and consciousness.

label: Biology
desc: The study of life in all its forms — from cells and genes to ecosystems and evolution.

label: Psychology
desc: The science of mind and behaviour — how humans perceive, think, feel, and act.
picks: Man's Search for Meaning — Viktor Frankl

label: Medicine
desc: The science and practice of healing the human body — from diagnostics to treatment to prevention.
picks: How to Change Your Mind — Michael Pollan

label: Mathematics
desc: The language of pattern and structure — the abstract discipline that underlies all of science.

label: Astrophysics
desc: The physics of stars, galaxies, and the cosmos — understanding the universe at the largest scales.

label: Ecology
desc: The study of relationships between living organisms and their environments — the science of interconnection.
picks: A Sand County Almanac — Aldo Leopold

label: Genetics
desc: The science of heredity and DNA — how traits are encoded, expressed, and passed on.

label: Chemistry
desc: The science of matter and its transformations — the bridge between physics and biology.

label: Artificial Intelligence
desc: Machine learning, neural networks, and the development of non-human intelligence — the most consequential technology of our time.
picks: Artificial Intelligence: A Guide for Thinking Humans — Melanie Mitchell

label: Blockchain
desc: Distributed ledger technology — trustless systems for recording transactions and governing without central authority.

label: Decentralized Networks
desc: Peer-to-peer architectures that remove single points of control — the infrastructure of a more open internet.

label: AR / VR
desc: Augmented and virtual reality — technologies blending the digital and physical to create new perceptual worlds.

label: Biotechnology
desc: Using biology as technology — engineering living systems to solve human problems.

label: Robotics
desc: The design and engineering of autonomous machines — from industrial robots to soft bio-inspired forms.
picks: How to Create a Mind — Ray Kurzweil

label: Web3
desc: A vision of a decentralised web owned by users rather than corporations — still being built, still contested.

label: Quantum Computing
desc: Computing that harnesses quantum mechanical phenomena — with the potential to solve problems beyond classical computers.

label: Cybersecurity
desc: The protection of digital systems, data, and people — increasingly critical as life moves online.

label: Music
desc: Sound organised into meaning — perhaps the most universal human art form.
picks: This Is Your Brain on Music — Daniel Levitin

label: Visual Art
desc: Painting, sculpture, installation, and image — art that speaks through what is seen.

label: Film
desc: Moving image as storytelling — cinema at its best as the collective dream of a culture.

label: Poetry
desc: Language at its most compressed and alive — where sound, image, and meaning converge.

label: Dance
desc: The body as instrument — movement as communication before language.

label: Architecture
desc: The art of shaping space — how buildings form the invisible container of human experience.
picks: The Architecture of Happiness — Alain de Botton

label: Fashion
desc: Clothing as culture, identity, and expression — where design meets the body.

label: Storytelling
desc: The ancient technology of narrative — how stories shape identity, culture, and what we believe is possible.

label: Photography
desc: The art of the decisive moment — freezing light and time into meaning.

label: Theater
desc: Live performance as ritual — the oldest form of communal storytelling.

label: Sacred Geometry
desc: The mathematical patterns underlying nature and cosmology — from the golden ratio to the Flower of Life.

label: Astrology
desc: The ancient map of cosmic rhythms — how planetary cycles reflect and inform human experience.

label: Climate
desc: The science and crisis of Earth's changing atmosphere — the most urgent challenge of this century.

label: Biodiversity
desc: The richness of life on Earth — the species, ecosystems, and genetic diversity we depend on and are losing.

label: Oceans
desc: The world's largest ecosystem — covering 71% of Earth and generating over half our oxygen.
picks: The Sea Around Us — Rachel Carson

label: Forests
desc: The lungs of the planet — ancient wood-wide webs of intelligence and interdependence.
picks: The Hidden Life of Trees — Peter Wohlleben

label: Cosmos
desc: The universe in its totality — its origin, structure, and our place within it.

label: Energy Fields
desc: The electromagnetic and subtle energetic phenomena that permeate living systems and space.

label: Natural Cycles
desc: The rhythms of moon, season, and breath — the patterns that govern organic life.
picks: The Order of Time — Carlo Rovelli

label: DeFi
desc: Decentralised Finance — financial instruments and markets built on blockchain, without banks.

label: Impact Investing
desc: Capital directed toward measurable social and environmental outcomes alongside financial returns.

label: Social Enterprise
desc: Business models that put mission first — companies designed to serve people and planet, not just profit.
picks: Let My People Go Surfing — Yvon Chouinard

label: Circular Economy
desc: Economic systems designed to eliminate waste — where the end of one product becomes the start of another.

label: Conscious Capital
desc: Investment and finance as a force for good — capital aligned with values, not just returns.

label: Regenerative Business
desc: Companies that give back more than they take — business as a restorative force in the world.

label: DAOs
desc: Decentralised Autonomous Organisations — internet-native entities governed by code and community rather than hierarchy.

label: Oneness
desc: The non-dual understanding that all existence is one — the mystical foundation underlying most wisdom traditions.
picks: The Power of Now — Eckhart Tolle

label: Source
desc: The origin of existence — the ground of being from which all phenomena arise.
picks: The Tao Te Ching — Lao Tzu

label: Soul
desc: The individual spark of consciousness — its nature, purpose, and relationship to the universal.
picks: Care of the Soul — Thomas Moore

label: Akashic Records
desc: The concept of a universal field containing the imprint of all events, thoughts, and experiences.
picks: Accessing the Akashic Records — Linda Howe

label: Dimensions
desc: Beyond the three spatial dimensions — higher-dimensional reality as explored through physics, mysticism, and consciousness.

label: Synchronicity
desc: Meaningful coincidence — Jung's concept of acausal connecting principle linking inner and outer events.

label: Sacred Law
desc: The universal principles governing existence — natural law as understood across indigenous, hermetic, and contemplative traditions.

label: Merkaba
desc: The light-body — sacred geometry applied to human energy fields and consciousness.
picks: The Ancient Secret of the Flower of Life — Drunvalo Melchizedek

label: Vibration
desc: Everything is frequency — the understanding that matter, thought, and emotion all operate as oscillating energy.

label: Nutrition
desc: Food as medicine — the science and art of nourishing the body optimally.
picks: How Not to Die — Michael Greger

label: Movement
desc: The body in motion — from exercise and sport to somatic practice and daily physical awareness.

label: Sleep
desc: The foundation of health and cognition — the science and practice of deep, restorative rest.

label: Plant Medicine
desc: Healing plants and fungi — from adaptogenic herbs to entheogenic ceremony.
picks: Plants of the Gods — Schultes & Hofmann

label: Community
desc: The art of living together — building belonging, trust, and shared life with others.

label: Minimalism
desc: Intentional reduction — creating space for what matters by releasing what doesn't.

label: Detox
desc: Clearing the body, mind, and environment of what burdens them — fasting, cleansing, and conscious simplification.

label: Sustainable Living
desc: Living within planetary limits — practical choices that reduce footprint and increase rootedness.

label: Product Design
desc: Designing objects, systems, and experiences people love to use — where function meets form.
picks: The Design of Everyday Things — Don Norman

label: Systems Thinking
desc: Seeing wholes, not parts — the discipline of understanding complex, interconnected systems.

label: Open Source
desc: Building in public — code, knowledge, and tools shared freely for anyone to use and improve.
picks: The Cathedral and the Bazaar — Eric Raymond

label: Co-creation
desc: Creating together — practices and processes that harness collective intelligence to build more than any individual could.
picks: The Art of Possibility — Zander & Zander

label: Prototyping
desc: Making to learn — the practice of building rough versions fast to discover what works.

label: Emergence
desc: How complex behaviour arises from simple rules — the property that makes life, consciousness, and cities possible.

label: Biomimicry
desc: Nature as design teacher — innovation inspired by 3.8 billion years of biological evolution.

label: Future Tech
desc: Technologies that don't fully exist yet — the horizon of what's coming next.

label: Meditation
desc: The practice of conscious attention — sitting with what is, training the mind toward presence.

label: Sound Healing
desc: Frequency as medicine — the use of vibration, music, and resonance to heal body and mind.
picks: The Healing Power of Sound — Mitchell Gaynor

label: Breathwork
desc: The breath as a tool — conscious breathing practices that regulate the nervous system and unlock altered states.

label: Somatic Therapy
desc: Healing stored in the body — trauma-informed approaches that work through sensation and movement rather than talk alone.

label: Energy Healing
desc: Working with the subtle body — reiki, acupuncture, and other modalities that address the energetic layer of health.

label: Yoga
desc: The union of body, breath, and mind — one of humanity's oldest and most refined movement-meditation systems.

label: Mental Health
desc: The full spectrum of psychological wellbeing — from everyday emotional literacy to clinical care and beyond.

label: Trauma
desc: The wound and the path through it — understanding how traumatic experience shapes the body, mind, and relationships.
picks: The Body Keeps the Score — Bessel van der Kolk

label: Governance
desc: How communities make decisions together — from ancient councils to digital democracy.

label: Indigenous Wisdom
desc: The knowledge systems of the world's original peoples — deep ecological intelligence developed across millennia.

label: Collective Intelligence
desc: The wisdom that emerges when many minds work together — swarm intelligence, distributed cognition, and shared sense-making.
picks: The Wisdom of Crowds — James Surowiecki

label: Social Movements
desc: Organised collective action for change — how societies transform through protest, organising, and cultural shift.
picks: This Is An Uprising — Engler & Engler

label: Peace
desc: More than the absence of war — active peace-building, reconciliation, and the conditions for human flourishing.

label: Future Cities
desc: Urban design for human and ecological thriving — the cities we need to build and the ones we need to transform.
picks: The Death and Life of Great American Cities — Jane Jacobs

label: Education Systems
desc: How societies pass knowledge to the next generation — and how those systems need to change.
picks: Sugata Mitra — Hole in the Wall

label: Diaspora
desc: Communities living away from their ancestral homelands — the cultures, identities, and networks that bridge worlds.

label: Language
desc: The system of meaning that makes humans human — and how the languages we speak shape the thoughts we can think.

label: Public Speaking
desc: The art of moving people with words — presence, clarity, and the power to shift a room.

label: Dialogue
desc: Conversation as a practice — creating the conditions for genuine mutual understanding.

label: Journalism
desc: Bearing witness and telling truth — the essential craft of documenting reality for the public good.
picks: The Elements of Journalism — Kovach & Rosenstiel

label: Social Media
desc: Digital platforms as public squares — and the profound effects they have on attention, identity, and collective sense-making.
picks: Ten Arguments for Deleting Your Social Media Accounts — Jaron Lanier

label: Nonviolent Communication
desc: Marshall Rosenberg's framework for compassionate connection — needs, feelings, and the language of empathy.

label: Translation
desc: The work of crossing linguistic borders — and what is always gained and lost in the crossing.

label: Broadcasting
desc: One-to-many communication — radio, television, podcasting, and the architecture of public attention.

label: Renewable Energy
desc: Energy from inexhaustible sources — wind, solar, hydro, and geothermal as the foundations of a post-carbon world.

label: Solar
desc: The sun as power source — photovoltaics and concentrated solar as the fastest-growing energy technology in history.

label: Wind Energy
desc: Capturing the motion of air — onshore and offshore wind as mature, scalable clean energy.

label: Geothermal
desc: Earth's internal heat as power — stable, baseload renewable energy from the planet itself.

label: Energy Storage
desc: Batteries, flywheels, and hydrogen — the technologies that make intermittent renewables reliable.

label: Bioenergy
desc: Energy from biological material — from biogas to advanced biofuels, biomass and beyond.

label: Grid Technology
desc: Smart grids, microgrids, and the infrastructure of energy distribution — how power gets from source to use.

label: Energy Policy
desc: The governance of energy systems — the laws, incentives, and international agreements shaping the energy transition.

label: Regenerative Farming
desc: Agriculture that rebuilds soil health, sequesters carbon, and restores ecosystems rather than depleting them.

label: Permaculture
desc: Design science for sustainable human habitats — mimicking the patterns and resilience of natural ecosystems.
picks: Permaculture: A Designers' Manual — Bill Mollison

label: Food Systems
desc: The full chain from soil to fork — understanding food production, distribution, and consumption as an integrated whole.

label: Urban Farming
desc: Growing food in cities — rooftop gardens, vertical farms, and community plots reclaiming urban space.

label: Fermentation
desc: The ancient art of transformation — microbes, cultures, and the living foods that feed the gut.
picks: The Art of Fermentation — Sandor Katz

label: Food Sovereignty
desc: The right of peoples to define their own food systems — local control over seeds, land, and the food supply.

label: Cuisine
desc: Cooking as culture — the traditions, flavours, and knowledge that different peoples have built around food.
picks: Salt, Fat, Acid, Heat — Samin Nosrat

label: Aquaculture
desc: Farming the water — the sustainable cultivation of fish, shellfish, algae, and other aquatic organisms.

label: Sports
desc: Physical competition as culture — athleticism, teamwork, and the stories we tell through games.
picks: The Inner Game of Tennis — W. Timothy Gallwey

label: Games & Gaming
desc: Video games as art, culture, and community — one of the defining media forms of the 21st century.

label: Outdoor Adventure
desc: The wild as playground — hiking, climbing, surfing, and the practice of moving through the natural world.
picks: The Last Child in the Woods — Richard Louv

label: Esports
desc: Competitive gaming at scale — the convergence of sport, entertainment, and digital culture.

label: Leisure & Rest
desc: The art of doing nothing — rest, idleness, and the case for a less productive life.
picks: In Praise of Idleness — Bertrand Russell

label: Humor & Comedy
desc: Laughter as wisdom — the way comedy reveals truth by approaching it from the side.

label: Festivals & Events
desc: Gathering in celebration — music festivals, cultural events, and the ancient human need for seasonal ritual.

label: Play & Creativity
desc: Play as the engine of innovation — how unstructured exploration leads to the most original thinking.

label: History
desc: The suppressed and forgotten chapters of human civilisation — lost cultures, ancient advanced knowledge, and the researchers challenging the official timeline.
picks: Fingerprints of the Gods — Graham Hancock

label: Extraterrestrial Life
desc: Evidence, contact, and the implications of non-human intelligence — from UAP disclosure to ancient astronaut theory and the search for life beyond Earth.
picks: UFOs and the National Security State — Richard Dolan

label: Channeling
desc: Receiving transmission from non-physical intelligence — guides, higher selves, and entities communicating through human vessels.
picks: The Law of Attraction — Esther & Jerry Hicks (Abraham)

label: Kriya Yoga
desc: The ancient science of energy and breath — the meditation techniques transmitted through Lahiri Mahasaya and Paramhansa Yogananda that accelerate spiritual evolution.
picks: Autobiography of a Yogi — Paramhansa Yogananda

label: Remote Viewing
desc: The scientifically studied ability to perceive distant targets through non-local consciousness — developed in US government intelligence programmes.

label: Manifestation
desc: The art of consciously creating reality — the principles, practices, and traditions behind turning thought into form.

label: Personal Development
desc: The inner and outer work of becoming — habits, mindset, communication, and the lifelong practice of self-mastery.
picks: Think and Grow Rich — Napoleon Hill

label: Prosperity Mindset
desc: Wealth as a state of consciousness first — the philosophy, psychology, and practical wisdom of financial abundance.
picks: The Richest Man in Babylon — George S. Clason

label: Near-Death Experiences
desc: Accounts and science of consciousness beyond physical death — what NDEs reveal about the soul, reality, and what awaits.

label: Siddha Yoga
desc: The path of grace through the Siddha lineage — shaktipat initiation, self-inquiry, and the teachings of Swami Muktananda and Gurumayi.

label: EFT & Tapping
desc: Emotional Freedom Techniques — combining acupressure tapping with modern psychology to release emotional blocks and reprogram limiting beliefs.

label: Decentralised AI
desc: AI infrastructure owned by networks, not corporations — protocols that coordinate machine intelligence, computation, and inference on open, censorship-resistant rails.
